title: Test filename suffixes carry meaning: .spec.ts for unit-style specs, .integration.spec.ts for...
learning: Test filename suffixes carry meaning: `*.spec.ts` for unit-style specs, `*.integration.spec.ts` for integration specs that boot Nest modules or hit a DB. Match the suffix to the test layer rather than to existing siblings.

title: When the same shaped object is constructed in more than one test with small field overrides,...
learning: When the same shaped object is constructed in more than one test with small field overrides, introduce a builder rather than copy-pasting literals. The repo's `Builder<T>.with(field, value)` pattern is the default for entity-like fixtures.

title: Before hardcoding a knob (limit, page cap, threshold), search the repo for the same concern i...
learning: Before hardcoding a knob (limit, page cap, threshold), search the repo for the same concern in another repository/manager. If a precedent exists (ChainsRepository, ContractsRepository), follow the configuration-driven pattern instead of introducing a hardcoded copy.

title: When adding a required field to the configuration schema, update every invalidConfiguration t...
learning: When adding a required field to the configuration schema, update every `invalidConfiguration` test fixture used by other tests, not only `validConfiguration`. Otherwise tests asserting on unrelated invalid fields start failing on the new field instead of the intended one.

title: Destructuring with the same name as an outer let variable shadows it.
learning: Destructuring with the same name as an outer `let` variable shadows it. `let next = null; const { next, results } = parsed` keeps the outer `next` at `null`. Either rename in the destructure (`const { next: nextUrl, ... }`) or reassign explicitly to the outer variable.

title: When paginating large objects but only one field flows downstream, extract that field per pag...
learning: When paginating large objects but only one field flows downstream, extract that field per page (push addresses, not whole `SafeV2`s) rather than accumulating full objects in memory across pages.

title: Migration runners must not use the recorded last migration id as an array index.
learning: Migration runners must not use the recorded last migration `id` as an array index. Once migrations get deleted and IDs become non-contiguous, `migrations.slice(lastId)` returns the wrong slice. Filter by `migration.id > lastId` instead, and add an integration test for the gap case.

title: Expiry-only caches need code comments
learning: Datasource methods with no invalidation hook should document that cached data lives until the configured expiration, so future maintainers do not assume active invalidation exists.

title: Architecture docs must allow real module exceptions
learning: Architecture/spec docs should describe the module shapes that actually exist, including versioned or optional folders, instead of enforcing a single strict tree that several features do not follow.

title: Normalize cached external payloads exactly once
learning: When external payloads are normalized before caching, cache-hit code must not normalize them again; tests should cover cache-hit and cache-miss paths for transformations with signs or other non-idempotent changes.

title: Internal boolean query params should be strict
learning: Internal API boolean query params should validate explicit `true`/`false` strings and default intentionally instead of accepting arbitrary strings and transforming every non-true value to false.

title: Migration metadata should preserve ownership semantics
learning: Campaign and data migrations should preserve ownership/reporting metadata from the existing campaign unless the ownership change is intentional and documented; copy-pasted template fields need review.

title: Cache format changes need versioning or invalidation
learning: Changing a cache payload format under the same key can crash or misroute cache-hit callers after deploy; version or invalidate the key, and do not silently return plausible empty data when cold-cache fetches fail.

title: Empty external error strings should normalize to absence
learning: When mapping optional external error strings, treat empty strings as absent if they carry no user-facing information, and keep the return type honest by returning `undefined` instead of an empty message.

title: Cache router fields should match service ownership
learning: Cache router helpers should not set misleading default fields when a service owns multiple hash fields; either pass the field explicitly or leave field selection to the service.

title: Unknown chain mappings should not default to plausible chains
learning: When an external chain mapping is missing, return absence or fall back to the non-provider path rather than defaulting to mainnet or zero balances that look valid.

title: Env renames need backward-compatible fallback
learning: Renaming an environment variable for clarity must keep the old variable as a fallback for at least one release when the PR claims backwards compatibility.

title: Provider-specific filters must be documented or removed
learning: If query parameters such as trusted/spam filters only apply on one provider path, the public contract must document that limitation or remove unsupported parameters from the endpoint.

title: Swagger names must match real standards and hide providers
learning: Public API docs should not name internal providers or claim a standard format such as CAIP-10 when the implementation accepts a different shape.

title: Portfolio totals preserve signed filtered data
learning: Portfolio dust filters should preserve negative balances by magnitude, and totals must be computed after dropping unsupported or invalid positions so aggregates match returned items.

title: Debug logs and internal errors stay out of production responses
learning: Temporary `console.log` statements and raw internal error messages must not ship in production or user-facing failure responses; keep generic user copy unless the error is intentionally mapped.

title: Schemas and DTOs should reuse exported enum contracts intentionally
learning: DTO/Swagger enum values and Zod literals should reuse canonical enum/status definitions where possible, and private intermediate schemas should not be exported unless consumers or tests use them.

title: Security analysis branches need edge-case tests
learning: Security analysis logic that extracts handlers or classifies official/unofficial contracts needs negative-path tests for wrong parameter names, invalid addresses, missing parameters, and official-safe false-positive paths.

title: Auth headers must be overrideable and non-leaking
learning: Network auth header injection should allow per-request overrides, avoid logging secrets directly or inside request objects, and keep cache keys/log output from exposing authorization material.

title: Config env tests restore process state and trim secrets
learning: Configuration validation tests that mutate `process.env.NODE_ENV` must restore it, and API-key validators should trim whitespace so blank strings cannot pass as configured secrets.

title: Drive-by test seed/race fixes split into their own PR
learning: When a feature PR also includes an unrelated test infrastructure fix (faker seed reset, race-condition shim, lint-only edits), split that fix into its own PR. The mixed scope hides whichever of the two the review actually verified, and bisecting later treats the feature commit as the cause of any regression in the unrelated cleanup.

title: Cache key includes the circuit-breaker or behavior-toggle flag
learning: When an in-flight request cache or memoizer is gated by a behavior toggle (circuit breaker on/off, trusted/spam filters, sync/async), the cache key must include that toggle. Otherwise a request issued with the toggle off populates the cache and a later call with the toggle on returns the cached promise, silently bypassing the very protection the toggle was meant to add.

title: Resilience policy semantics: classify failures, scope keys, do not count blocked attempts
learning: Circuit breakers, retries, and rate limiters need careful semantic design. Failure classification must enumerate every failure type the policy intends to count (network/timeout errors, not just HTTP 5xx). Policy keys are scoped per-service (hostname or service base URL), not per full URL, so an outage of one upstream trips the breaker for that upstream and not for every endpoint independently. Policy-blocked attempts (CircuitBreakerException) are not recorded as policy failures - that conflates blocked traffic with downstream failure and prevents stale circuits from cleaning up. Stale-cleanup math distinguishes absolute timestamps from durations: multiply durations, never timestamps. Error wrapping at the policy boundary stays consistent regardless of whether the policy is enabled (URL parsing still surfaces as the framework's NetworkRequestError, not a raw TypeError).

title: .env.sample comments document encoding and format constraints
learning: When an env var has a non-obvious encoding or format (base64, URL-encoded, comma-separated, signed JWT), document that constraint in the `.env.sample` comment alongside the variable, not only in a separate README. Operators copy `.env.sample` into local files and only consult the README when something already broke.

title: Use safe-deployments for version lists, do not hardcode
learning: When code needs the set of supported Safe contract versions or fallback handler versions, derive it from `@safe-global/safe-deployments` rather than hardcoding the array. The deployments package is the single source of truth and is updated on every contract release; a hardcoded list silently lags behind.

title: Do not export symbols that are not consumed externally
learning: Module-local constants, schemas, or helpers that no other module imports should not be `export`ed. Exporting them expands the public surface and invites accidental coupling; promote to an export only when a real consumer or test exists.

title: Push negative paths to the lowest layer; e2e proves wiring once per route, not per branch
learning: When adding negative-path coverage for a new endpoint, route to the lowest layer that can prove the property: zod union/`.strict()` rejections belong in a schema unit spec; service/repo branches (auth assertions, affected=0, error mapping) belong in service/repo unit specs; one representative 4xx mapping smoke per route at e2e is enough to cover the global exception filter wiring. Stacking the same negative scenarios at unit, integration, and e2e duplicates effort and slows CI without adding signal. Concrete trigger: a PR added 403/404/422×2 negative cases at e2e for a new resend endpoint, but each one had a strictly cheaper home — repo unit spec for the branch logic, schema unit spec for the zod rejections — and the existing happy-path e2e already covered the wiring/filter-mapping smoke claim.

title: Space-scoped queue endpoint shipped without auth and membership checks
learning: A new space-scoped queued-transactions endpoint initially had no `@UseGuards(AuthGuard)` and did not assert that the caller was a member of the space before returning space-owned transaction data. Space routes must copy the existing spaces pattern: guard the controller, pass the auth payload through, and assert membership or admin status before data access.

title: Address-or-email invite DTOs should be union schemas
learning: The invite payload accepted exactly one of address or email, but the first implementation modeled that with optional fields and refinement. Review pushed it to a Zod union of strict address and email object shapes, making the wire contract, validation errors, and downstream TypeScript narrowing match the behavior.

title: Invite-expiry migration backfilled declined rows against runtime invariant
learning: A migration backfilled `inviteExpiresAt` for both INVITED and DECLINED member rows while the runtime decline path explicitly clears the expiration. Status migrations must preserve the invariant for each status and use the narrowest predicate needed, not raw enum ordinals that make unrelated statuses look eligible.

title: Mutable test builder reused across cases leaked state between assertions
learning: A spec reused one `multisigTransactionBuilder` across five `mapCustomTransaction` cases. `Builder.with()` mutates and returns `this`, so each `.with(...)` permanently changed state for later cases — the assertions passed for the wrong reasons and the test names misdescribed what was exercised. Build a fresh builder per case (or per assertion); never reuse a mutable builder across cases, since `.with()` is not a pure copy.

title: Strict hex equality gave false mismatches without case normalization
learning: Relay validation compared `stored.data` against viem's `decoded.data` with `!==`. viem emits lowercase hex, but the stored payload is only shape-validated (`NullableHexSchema`) and mixed-case hex is legal, so a case difference produced a false mismatch and rejected a legitimate relay. Normalize both sides (`.toLowerCase()`) before strict equality whenever comparing hex/address-like strings that may differ only in case.

title: Normalize and brand validated value types at the schema boundary
learning: Email normalization (`.trim().toLowerCase()`) was repeated at every repository call site, and the new `EmailAddress` type was a bare `z.infer` alias (plain `string`) so any unnormalized string could be assigned without going through the schema. Normalize once at the validation boundary via a Zod `.toLowerCase()`/`.transform()` so consumers receive ready-to-use values, and `.brand<'X'>()` validated value types so the type system enforces that a value actually passed through the schema rather than being a same-shaped raw string.

title: Query method placed in the route service instead of the repository
learning: `findActiveSurveyOrFail` (a DB lookup) was defined on the route service. Methods that issue database queries belong in the repository layer; the service orchestrates and the repository owns persistence access. Keep query/`*OrFail` lookups in the repository so the boundary between orchestration and data access stays clean.

title: forwardRef used without an actual circular dependency
learning: A new module wrapped an import in `forwardRef(() => AuthModule)` although there was no circular dependency between the modules. `forwardRef` should only be used to break a genuine import cycle; adding it speculatively hides the real dependency graph and invites confusion. Drop it unless a cycle actually exists.

title: Upsert followed by a redundant find instead of using its returned rows
learning: An upsert was followed by a separate `findOneOrFail` to read back generated columns. `repo.upsert()` already returns generated/default columns (e.g. `submittedAt`/`updatedAt`) in `result.generatedMaps`, so the extra round-trip was unnecessary. Read generated values from the write's RETURNING/`generatedMaps` instead of issuing a follow-up query.

title: Controller coverage written as e2e, which does not run in CI
learning: New controller coverage was added as an e2e spec, which only runs on demand and not as part of CI, so the behavior was effectively unverified on every PR. Put coverage that must run on every CI run in an integration spec; reserve e2e specs for the on-demand suite. Match the test layer (and filename suffix) to where it should execute.

title: Migration address join must normalize case on both sides
learning: A migration joined `wallets.address = members.invited_by` with raw equality, but `wallets.address` is stored checksummed via `databaseAddressTransformer`/`getAddress()` while `members.invited_by` may carry different casing. The preflight check then flagged same-address rows as unresolvable and aborted the migration. When a migration compares address columns where one side passes through a checksum transformer, normalize both sides (`LOWER(...)`) so equal addresses match regardless of stored case.

title: TypeORM array where dropped the user filter, leaking other spaces
learning: Refactoring a single-object `where` into an array (`findSpaces`) introduced an authorization bypass: TypeORM treats array entries as OR, and one clause omitted the `user: { id: userId }` filter, so any authenticated user could read another space by id (and an undefined `spaceId` matched every member row). When converting an object `where` to an array, remember each entry is an independent OR branch — every clause must carry the ownership/membership predicate. Centralize the shared predicate in a helper (`activeOrPendingMemberWhere`) and add a non-member-requesting-a-spaceId regression test.

title: Find-or-create ran outside the outer transaction, leaking orphan rows
learning: Inside `inviteUsers`, the wallet branch called `findOrCreateByWalletAddress(address, 'PENDING')` without the outer `EntityManager`, so the user+wallet creation ran in its own transaction. When the later per-item `DbMember` insert failed, the outer transaction rolled back the member but the freshly created `User`/`Wallet` rows stayed committed — orphan PENDING rows on every failed invite. Multi-step writes that must commit/roll back together must share the outer transaction's `EntityManager`; methods used inside a transaction should accept and thread an optional `EntityManager` rather than opening their own.

title: DTO Zod schema was looser than its documented @ApiProperty bounds
learning: An invite DTO validated `name` with `z.string().max(255)` while its `@ApiProperty` advertised `minLength: 3`/`maxLength: 30` — so the runtime accepted empty/oversized names the OpenAPI contract forbade, and the DTO class could drift from the Zod schema. Make the DTO's Zod schema match the documented `@ApiProperty` bounds by reusing the canonical schema (`makeNameSchema()`), and have the DTO class `implements z.infer<typeof Schema>` so schema/Swagger drift fails at compile time. Drop `export` on DTO classes only referenced within their own file.

title: Find-or-create helper name promised a status the method does not guarantee
learning: `findOrCreatePendingByEmail` returned the existing user id even when that user was ACTIVE — PENDING was only the create-path status, not a postcondition — so callers skim-reading the signature could gate behavior on a status the method never guaranteed. It was renamed to `findOrCreateByEmail` (matching `findOrCreateByWalletAddress`). Do not encode a status or other postcondition in a helper name unless the method guarantees it on every path.

title: Admin fixtures seeded invitedBy with random ints instead of null
learning: Integration fixtures seeded space admins with `invitedBy: faker.number.int(...)`, but an admin is never invited by anyone — the value should be `null`. Nothing asserted on the field, so tests passed, but the fixture misrepresented domain semantics and misleads future readers. Fixture values must reflect domain reality even when unasserted.

title: Sequential awaits for independent membership lookups
learning: A route service awaited `findAuthorizedMembersOrFail` and then `findActiveAdmin` sequentially even though the two lookups are independent. Run independent repository/API lookups in one `Promise.all` so the request path pays a single round-trip latency.

title: Inline max-length literal and entity limit changed without a migration
learning: A DTO schema inlined `makeNameSchema({ maxLength: 255 })` instead of a named constant shared with peer entities, and tightening it to the entity's 30-char limit had to be reverted because the entity code claimed 30 while no migration had ever changed the column — the DB still allowed more. Validation limits are named constants shared between schema and entity, and an entity column limit changes only together with the migration that enforces it.

title: Schema-centralization refactor silently changed the wire contract
learning: A refactor that centralized field rules via `Schema.shape.<field>` accidentally made a required DTO field optional (`.optional()` added during the move) and missed a sibling entity that should have received the same `ChainIdSchema` tightening; both were fixed after review. When centralizing or reusing shared schema shapes, preserve requiredness and bounds exactly, sweep sibling entities for the same field, and call out any intentional behavior change in the PR description.

title: Simulation persistence flag defaulted to saving every payload
learning: A Tenderly simulation request copied `save: true` from the web client, persisting every relay simulation (Safe addresses + calldata) on a third-party surface. Review changed it to `save_if_fails` only. Flags that persist request payloads to external services default to the least-retentive option that still supports debugging.

title: Nested ternaries for error formatting instead of a small utils helper
learning: Error-message construction used ternary-of-ternary template literals inline in a datasource; review asked twice to extract it into a small helper with plain `if`s in a utils file. Message/formatting logic beyond one branch gets a named helper — nested ternaries in template literals are a readability flag.

title: Lifecycle status check placed in the repository instead of the service
learning: A repository method checked `member.status !== 'INVITED'` and threw `ConflictException` before its write. Business-rule gating (which lifecycle states allow an operation) belongs in the service layer; repositories persist, query, and map constraint errors. The boundary cuts both ways: services stay out of persistence internals, repositories stay out of business rules.

title: Single-statement write wrapped in an unnecessary transaction
learning: An invite-renewal update wrapped one UPDATE in `postgresDatabaseService.transaction(...)`. A single statement is already atomic; explicit transactions are for multi-step writes that must commit or roll back together. Do not add transaction plumbing around a lone write.

title: Numeric-string ID schema accepted zero and relied on loose shared schema
learning: A chainId response field moved from `z.number()` to a numeric string; review iterations settled on `/^[1-9]\d*$/` because `^\d+$` accepts `"0"` and leading zeros, and the shared `ChainIdSchema` was too loose (negatives, floats) to reuse without a separate fix. Test builders had to generate conforming values too: `faker.string.numeric()` allows leading zeros, so `faker.number.int(...).toString()` was used instead. Numeric-string ID schemas reject zero, leading zeros, signs, and floats, and test-data generators must satisfy the same constraint.

title: Conditionally required env vars added as standalone checks that also fired locally
learning: A new deployed-env requirement (`AWS_WEB_IDENTITY_TOKEN_FILE` when SES email is enabled) was first added as a separate `superRefine` check plus local-env validation, defeating the existing deployed-env-only guard. Review consolidated it into the shared deployed-env required-fields list with a `requiredWhen` condition and per-field message. Conditionally required env vars extend that single list — never standalone checks that also fire in local dev — and values a feature needs once enabled are read with `getOrThrow` so startup fails fast instead of failing at send time.

title: Default-off feature module made @Global and wired into the shared test module
learning: A default-off SES email module was registered in the shared testing module and later marked `@Global()` to avoid per-test wiring — review called both the wrong fix: the rest of the app does not need the module, and `@Global` includes it in every initialization. Keep default-off feature modules scoped: import them only where used and add test-module overrides only in the specs that exercise them.

title: Generic email-queue service injected directly into a domain service
learning: `MembersService` injected the generic `SesEmailQueueService` directly; review asked for a feature-specific middleman (`SpaceInviteEmailService`) that owns email preparation and queue dispatch for that flow. Domain services depend on a feature-scoped orchestration service, not on generic infrastructure services (email, queues) directly — the middleman keeps templates, config, and dispatch concerns out of the domain flow.

title: Environment-specific invite URL hardcoded as a constant
learning: An email template hardcoded `SPACE_INVITE_URL = 'http://localhost:3001/...'` with a TODO; review blocked the merge until it was configurable. Environment-specific URLs (like TTLs and other tunables) go through configuration from the first commit — a TODO does not ship.

title: Named-receiver convention requested but destructuring is the established pattern
learning: A review requested `const member = ...` over destructuring (`const { name } = ...`) as a claimed convention; a repo-wide check showed destructuring in ~79 non-test files across services, datasources, mappers, repositories, and controllers, and the request was withdrawn. Destructuring return values is an accepted CGW pattern — do not flag it, and verify a claimed convention against the codebase before requesting changes based on it.

title: UUID fields typed as plain string instead of the nominal UUID type
learning: A new `uuid` field was typed `string`; review switched it to the nominal `UUID` type from `node:crypto` so `Space['uuid']` is a distinct type and lookups like `findIdByUuid(uuid: Space['uuid'])` are type-checked end to end. Use the nominal `UUID` type for UUID-shaped fields, not `string`.

title: Identifier-format validation placed in the repository instead of a pipe
learning: A space-id lookup validated numeric/UUID format inside the repository (throwing `BadRequestException` from the data layer), and the two id pipes returned different 400 bodies for the same error class. The follow-up moved format checks into the pipes with one shared error-message constant; the repository assumes validated input. Param-format validation belongs in pipes/controllers with a single stable error contract — repositories should not throw HTTP exceptions for format errors.

title: TypeORM UPDATE does not populate generatedMaps
learning: A review suggested reading `result.generatedMaps[0]` instead of `result.raw[0]` after an update with `.returning(['id', 'uuid'])`, but TypeORM only fills `generatedMaps` for INSERT — for UPDATE it stays empty and tests failed, so the code went back to `raw`. Read RETURNING values from `generatedMaps` on inserts and from `raw` on updates.

title: Type-preserving normalization used .transform() instead of .overwrite()
learning: A chainIds dedup used `.transform((ids) => Array.from(new Set(ids)))`; review pointed at Zod v4's `.overwrite(...)`, which expresses type-preserving normalization without turning the schema into a pipeline (keeps introspection and the input type intact). Prefer `.overwrite()` over `.transform()` when the normalization does not change the type.

title: Pagination links built from the raw cursor instead of the clamped values
learning: An audit-log endpoint clamped `limit` to 100 and `offset` to ≥0 for the query, but built `next`/`previous` from the raw route URL — so `?cursor=limit=500` returned 100 rows with `next: null`, silently truncating the feed. The fix normalizes the cursor with the existing `setCursor`/`PaginationData` helper before building links, plus a unit test for the oversized-cursor case. Page links must derive from the clamped/normalized pagination values actually used in the query, never the raw client cursor.

title: Lowercase transform stacked on the already-checksumming AddressSchema
learning: An audit payload schema wrapped `AddressSchema` in a `.toLowerCase()` transform, but `AddressSchema` already validates and returns the EIP-55 checksummed form via `getAddress()`. The transform was dropped; payloads store the checksummed address. Do not re-normalize what the canonical schema already normalizes — store addresses in the checksummed form `AddressSchema` produces.

title: Implementation widened the interface's narrow parameter type
learning: A diff helper took `QueryDeepPartialEntity<Space>` although the interface already declared `Partial<Pick<Space, 'name' | 'status'>>`, forcing extra branching; an inline anonymous return shape also duplicated an existing schema's payload. Keep implementation signatures as narrow as the interface declares, and derive payload type aliases from the schema (`z.infer<typeof Schema>['payload']`) instead of restating the shape.

title: Empty comma-separated query param produced a confusing validation error
learning: `?event_type=` failed with `Invalid event type ""` because `''.split(',')` yields `[""]`. The fix filters empties so an empty param behaves like an omitted one. When parsing comma-separated query params, `.split(',').filter(Boolean)` (or short-circuit on empty) — the empty-string case always exists.

title: ApiPropertyOptional used for an always-present nullable field
learning: A DTO field that is always present in the response (explicitly `null` when absent semantically) was decorated `@ApiPropertyOptional`, which claims the key itself can be missing. The fix is `@ApiProperty({ nullable: true })` — required-but-nullable. Choose the decorator by the actual wire shape: optional means the key may be absent; nullable means the key is always there and may be `null`. The same mistake recurred with a chain `relayer` field that is always present but nullable (#3105). Conversely, genuinely optional fields must be `@ApiPropertyOptional`, not `@ApiProperty` (#3200).

title: Role-authorization test used a pending member, conflating two denial factors
learning: Tests meant to prove a non-admin MEMBER is rejected used an invited (pending) member; once content access became ACTIVE-only, the 403/404 could stem from the pending status rather than the missing ADMIN role, reducing the tests' signal. Negative authorization tests must isolate exactly one denial factor — an ACTIVE non-admin for role checks, a pending member for status checks.

title: Test helpers named by action instead of the membership state they produce
learning: E2e helpers `inviteMember`/`invitePendingMember`/`inviteActiveMember` needed explanatory comments and renaming rounds; the review settled on `createPendingMember`/`createActiveMember` — named for the state they produce, making the comments unnecessary — and on using the same wording ("pending member") across test descriptions. Name test helpers after the resulting state and keep terminology consistent across specs.

title: Status-narrowed WHERE turned an authorization failure into a 404
learning: `updateAlias` narrowed its WHERE to `status: 'ACTIVE'`, so a pending invitee fell into the `affected === 0` branch and received `NotFoundException` (404) while every sibling path rejected pending callers with 403 via `assertMember`. The fix asserts membership first, then updates — the authorization rule lives in one place and the status code stays consistent. Do not encode authorization in a WHERE clause whose empty result surfaces as 404.

title: New recursive calldata walker written next to an existing one; missing negative cache assertions
learning: A nested-`multiSend` `approveHash` walker initially handled only one nesting level; review pointed at the existing recursive decoded-transaction helper in `safe-shield/utils/transaction-mapping.utils.ts`, whose shape was adapted. The added tests also closed a negative-assertion gap: cache-invalidation tests must assert that unrelated Safes' caches stay untouched, not only that the targeted caches are cleared. Search for existing recursive transaction walkers before writing one, and pair invalidation tests with not-invalidated assertions.

title: Config-derived Zod schema rebuilt on every login request
learning: `buildSiweMessageSchema(this.clockSkewSeconds)` ran inside the verify method on every login although the config value never changes per instance; review moved the schema construction to the constructor. Objects derived only from constructor-time config (Zod schemas, clients, compiled regexes) are built once in the constructor, not per request.

title: CI node_modules cache silently skipped supply-chain integrity verification
learning: A CI speed-up cached `node_modules` keyed on `yarn.lock` and skipped `yarn install --immutable` on a hit — which also skipped `enableHardenedMode` supply-chain verification in jobs holding secrets, and opened a cache-tampering surface. The fix removed the `node_modules` cache and kept `setup-node`'s `cache: 'yarn'` download cache with an unconditional `yarn install --immutable`: tarball downloads stay cached (the expensive part) while integrity verification runs on every job. Do not cache linked `node_modules` in CI; cache the package-manager download cache and always install.

title: Dropping the build step removed PR-wide type checking and unloaded migrations
learning: Replacing `yarn build` (`generate-abis && nest build`) with just `generate-abis` silently lost whole-program type checking on PRs — ts-jest only type-checks files transitively imported by tests — and broke integration tests, which load migrations from `dist/migrations/*.js`. The fix added `yarn tsc --noEmit -p tsconfig.build.json` to the lint job (same scope `nest build` covered) and restored `yarn build` for the integration-tests job only. When touching CI build steps, keep a whole-program type check on PRs and know which jobs consume `dist/`.

title: Removed env vars left behind in .env.sample.json and devcontainer compose
learning: Moving relayer selection from env vars into per-chain config left `RELAY_DAILY_LIMIT_CHAIN_IDS`, `RELAY_FEE_CHAIN_IDS`, and `RELAY_SIMULATION_CHAIN_IDS` behind in `.env.sample.json` and `.devcontainer/docker-compose.yml`. When removing env-driven behavior, sweep every env-metadata surface — `.env.sample`, `.env.sample.json`, devcontainer compose files — in the same PR.

title: Redundant .default() stacked on .catch() and repeated fallback fragments
learning: A schema declared `z.boolean().catch(false).default(false)` on three fields; `.catch(false)` already handles undefined, null, and invalid values, so `.default(false)` is dead weight — and the repeated fragment was extracted to a shared `booleanWithFalseFallback` const. Do not stack `.default(x)` after `.catch(x)`, and extract repeated schema fallback fragments.

title: Not-implemented relayer type returned 403 instead of 501
learning: `RelayerTypeNotImplementedError` extended `ForbiddenException` (403) although the state is "the server has not implemented this relayer type yet", not a permission denial; the fix maps it to 501 Not Implemented and narrows the constructor's `relayerType: string` parameter to the enum. Pick the status code by what the state actually is, and type error-constructor params with the enum callers pass.

title: Builder default randomized across routing-relevant values, seeding flaky tests
learning: `relayerBuilder` defaulted `type` to `faker.helpers.arrayElement([...Object.values(RelayerType), null])`, so any test using `chainBuilder().build()` without overriding the field randomly routed to different relayer branches (403 vs 501 vs happy path) — a flaky-test source. Builder defaults must not randomize across semantically different, behavior-routing values; pick one stable valid default and override explicitly per test case.

title: Same field validated at two layers gave inconsistent 400/422 error contracts
learning: A new `nonce` field was validated both by a class-validator-style layer (`nonce=-12` → 400 with one message shape) and a Zod schema (`nonce=0x12` → 422 with another), so clients saw two different error contracts for the same field; manual testing caught it and the author unified the validation. Validate a field once, at one boundary. For simple shapes prefer `z.string().regex(/^\d+$/)` over a custom `.refine(predicate)` — same behavior, less code.

title: Portfolios fetched before Safe validation, spending shared rate-limit budget
learning: The overview flow prefetched Zerion portfolios before `safeRepository.getSafe` rejected invalid entries, so a request containing a non-Safe address spent a billed Zerion call and consumed the shared rate-limit budget for a result that was dropped. The fix validates every Safe up front and builds wallet identities only from validated entries. Validate/filter request entries before spending shared external-API budget on them — ordering matters once a global budget exists.

title: New rate-limit error swallowed by a catch that only rethrew ZodError
learning: Moving the collectibles path onto the shared Zerion rate limiter placed `assertWithinBudget` inside an existing try/catch whose catch only rethrew `ZodError` and wrapped everything else via `httpErrorFactory.from(...)` — so an over-budget request surfaced as 503 instead of the intended 429 (balances/positions explicitly preserved `LimitReachedError`). When adding a throwing call inside an existing try/catch, extend the catch's rethrow list so intentional domain errors keep their status.

title: Status union whose variants changed no reader behavior
learning: A `PortfolioResult` union (`{status:'ok',portfolio}` / `{status:'degraded'}`) was stored in a map although the only reader treated a degraded result and a missing key identically, and logging/metrics already happened at the failure site. The union was dropped for a plain `Map<string, Portfolio>` where a failed fetch is simply a missing key. Do not model states whose variants change no reader's behavior — absence can encode failure when nothing distinguishes them.

title: Chained refines recomputed values and scattered validation
learning: A name schema chained multiple `.refine(...)`s, each re-spreading the string to count grapheme length; review consolidated them into one `superRefine` with a shared `getNameLength` helper so all issues are collected in a single pass. Multi-condition validation uses one `superRefine`, not a chain of `.refine`s that duplicate computation and scatter messages.

title: Invisible-character stripping broke legitimate scripts and smart punctuation
learning: A Trojan-Source-motivated sanitizer stripped all `\p{Cf}` codepoints — including ZWJ/ZWNJ, which are required for correct Indic/Arabic/Persian name rendering — and the allowlist rejected the curly apostrophe (`O’Brien`) and en-dash that platform keyboards auto-insert; an all-invisibles input also got a misleading min-length message. The fixes: document (or narrow) the Cf stripping to the dangerous subset (bidi controls), fold smart punctuation to ASCII (`’`→`'`, `–`→`-`) before validation, and give "empty after sanitization" its own ordered message. Unicode sanitization must weigh legitimate-script collateral, not just the attack vector.

title: Builder invariant depended on faker locale behavior instead of construction
learning: `nameBuilder`'s spec asserted `sanitizeName(name) === name` on `faker.person.fullName()` output — true for the default locale today, but a Faker/locale change would make it flaky. The fix runs `sanitizeName` inside the builder before padding/slicing, so the output satisfies the invariant by construction. Builders enforce their own invariants internally; tests must not depend on incidental properties of faker data.

title: Vitest 4 semantics surfaced masked assertions and hoisting hazards
learning: The Jest→Vitest migration surfaced durable facts for this repo: Vitest 4's `toThrow(errorInstance)` deep-compares enumerable props (Jest compared only `.message`), which exposed a real 422-vs-502 discrepancy that message-only assertions now deliberately paper over (tracked in-thread); `vi.mock` factories referencing outer consts belong in `vi.hoisted(...)`; `vi.resetAllMocks()` restores spy originals in Vitest 4 (unlike Jest, where it neutered them); and docs must not carry exact test counts that drift (removed from TESTING.md). Also: review replies claimed fixes that were never committed — verify the diff before saying "fixed".

title: Deprecated-endpoint removal asked to cover the whole family
learning: A PR removing a deprecated `/raw` endpoint was asked to remove all `/raw` endpoints in the same PR. When retiring a deprecated surface, sweep the entire family in one change — a partial removal leaves an inconsistent API and a second cleanup PR.

title: Local chainId regex forked because the shared schema was too loose
learning: A GTF fee schema re-declared `z.coerce.string().regex(/^[1-9]\d*$/)` because the shared `NonNegativeNumericStringSchema` allowed leading zeros and `ChainIdSchema` allowed negatives/decimals. The resolution: tighten the shared `NonNegativeNumericStringSchema` (reject leading zeros, with tests) and reuse it via `.pipe()` plus a zero-refine — and defer the wider `ChainIdSchema` consolidation to its own PR. When a shared schema is too loose, tighten it with tests instead of forking a local copy; scope broad consolidations separately.

title: Inferred type split into a separate file from its schema
learning: A new entity file contained only `export type GtfFeesRequest = z.infer<typeof GtfFeesRequestSchema>` while the schema lived elsewhere; review asked to keep the schema and its inferred type together in the same `*.entity.ts`, matching the repo convention. Do not split a schema from its `z.infer` type.

title: Cache-key builder duplicated an enum default as a bare string
learning: Cache-dir builders used `args.origin ?? 'NATIVE'` while the datasource used `Origin.NATIVE` — if the enum value ever changes, the cache key silently diverges from the request actually sent. Fixed by importing the enum in the cache router, plus tests asserting that differing `nonce`/`origin`/`to` inputs produce differing cache keys. Cache-key builders share the datasource's constants, and key composition gets difference tests.

title: Outbound request stripping applied in one flow but not its sibling
learning: The new GTF flow parsed its outbound request through `GtfFeesRequestSchema` to strip fields outside the endpoint contract, but the sibling relay flow still forwarded `{...args.request, origin}` unfiltered — a DTO growth would silently leak fields upstream (and into cache keys). Also, a response class spread a wider object into its constructor and relied on the constructor to drop extras; review asked for explicit field picking. Parse outbound requests through the endpoint schema in every flow, and pick fields explicitly instead of spreading wider objects.

title: Fourth feature gate rejected on an already multi-gated Zerion path
learning: A PR wiring `chain.balancesProvider.enabled` into Zerion routing added a fourth gate to a path already controlled by a global flag, a chain-name mapping, and a per-chain endpoint flag; review pushed to consolidate and simplify balances gating instead of adding another flag (one added branch was also dead code — the wallet never queries balances for counterfactual Safes). The PR was closed unmerged. Do not stack another feature gate onto an already multi-gated path — consolidate the existing gates, and verify a new branch is actually reachable.

title: Randomly generated invalid input was occasionally valid, flaking the test
learning: A rate-limit guard spec used `faker.string.sample(10)` as an "invalid IP", but a random sample can occasionally parse as a valid IPv4/IPv6, making the rejection assertion flake; the fix uses a guaranteed-invalid literal. Fixtures meant to be invalid must be invalid by construction — a literal or a constructive generator — never a random sample that is only usually invalid.

title: Per-event handler log emitted at info instead of debug
learning: A cache-invalidation handler logged at `info` on every transaction event; review pointed out the AMQP event volume would flood Datadog and it moved to `debug`. Logs emitted once per queue event are `debug` — reserve `info` for low-frequency lifecycle signals.

title: Cache-invalidation fan-out rethrew on partial failure the caller cannot act on
learning: `ZerionCacheService.invalidate` ran three `deleteByKey` calls under `Promise.all`, so one rejection propagated to callers that cannot act on it (the AMQP listener will not replay the event; the wallet should not get a 500 for a failed invalidation whose entry expires with its TTL). Reworked to `Promise.allSettled` with a per-key warn, never throwing. Also: the invalidation source param was typed as a union (`TransactionEventType | 'refresh'`) instead of bare `string`, the method renamed to state its real blast radius (`clearZerionCaches`), and the integration test became an `it.each` over all seven invalidating event types. Best-effort invalidation semantics, honest names, and full event-type coverage.

title: Mocks double-cast through unknown and asserted via .mock.calls
learning: Three PRs in one week typed test mocks as `{...} as unknown as MockedObject<T>` and reached into `.mock.calls[i][j]` for assertions; review asked each time for a direct `as MockedObject<T>` cast (the `unknown` hop is redundant and hides missing method fakes) and `toHaveBeenNthCalledWith(...)` over `.mock.calls` indexing. Type mocks as `MockedObject<T>` directly and assert calls with the matcher API.

title: Two modules, config sections, and namespaces for one upstream billing service
learning: A billing API client added a `safeBillingService` config section next to the existing `billing` one, a second module wired alongside `BillingModule`, and mixed `SAFE_BILLING_*`/`BILLING_*` cache-key prefixes; review asked to consolidate — config sections use the topic name (no `-service` suffix, like `transactions`), the two modules were merged, and the namespaces unified. One upstream service maps to one module, one config section, and one namespace prefix.

title: Inline z.enum literals instead of as-const arrays shared with builders
learning: Subscription/plan schemas inlined literal arrays in `z.enum([...])`; review asked for `export const SubscriptionStatus = [...] as const` consumed as `z.enum([...SubscriptionStatus])` — the same array then feeds `faker.helpers.arrayElement` in test builders. Declare enum value sets once as `as const` arrays shared by schema and builders.

title: Network calls typed <unknown> where the expected shape was known
learning: A billing datasource called `networkService.post<unknown>` / `dataSource.get<unknown>` and cast later; review asked to carry the generic through (the helper already receives the Zod schema, so the type parameter can be derived) instead of `unknown`. Thread the expected type through datasource generics and validate with the schema — bare `<unknown>` without an adjacent parse is a flag.

title: Staging URL shipped as the production default for a new base URI
learning: A new service client defaulted `baseUri` to the staging host, so a production deploy with the env var missing would silently send a production token to staging. The fix adds the base URI to the deployed-env `requiredWhen` config check. Never default a base URI to a staging host — deployed environments must require it explicitly so misconfiguration fails at boot.

title: Identifier embedded in the cache-key prefix instead of the CacheDir field
learning: Billing cache keys interpolated the id into the key prefix (`${planId}_billing_plan`) and mixed two prefixes, so bulk invalidation by shared prefix (`prefix:*`) was impossible; the fix normalizes on one prefix with the identifier as the `CacheDir` field. Keys share one scannable prefix per resource; identifiers go in the field, not the prefix.

title: Interface demanded for a single-implementation auth service; interface justified for a disableable KMS service
learning: A review asked a webhook guard to inject an interface like `AuthGuard` does; the author kept the concrete `BillingAuthService` — it only verifies JWTs, has no data access, has exactly one plausible implementation, and `CaptchaGuard` sets the precedent. A suggestion to merge it into the generic `AuthGuard` was also declined: the similarity was superficial and merging couples two security paths. In the same window, `KmsService` DID gain an `IKmsService` interface — justified because a disabled-mode no-op implementation replaced a `client: undefined` construct-anyway workaround. Interfaces earn their place with a real second implementation or boundary, not by convention.

title: Test values: literals where faker belongs, randomization demanded where a contract constant belongs
learning: Auth-service specs hardcoded issuer strings and expiry days that are free inputs (faker belongs there), while a request to randomize the token role was correctly declined — `SERVICE_ACCESS` is a mint-time contract constant, not an input; the test instead asserts against the exported constant rather than a string literal. Randomize free inputs with faker; assert contract constants via their exported symbol; never randomize what the contract pins.

title: Bulk scan call and its tunables placed outside the provider's API service
learning: A malicious-address scanner instantiated its own Blockaid client and hardcoded `SCAN_TIMEOUT_MS`/`MAX_BATCH`/`CACHE_TTL_SECONDS`; review moved the bulk scan into `BlockaidApi` (all Blockaid logic in one place, no separate client provider) and the tunables into the config service — with the follow-up note that ideally business-specific tunables live in the feature service and are passed in, keeping the generic client free of business specifics. Provider calls belong in the provider's API service; tunables come from config, owned by the feature.

title: Ad-hoc config-service mocks where FakeConfigurationService exists
learning: New specs built ad-hoc `getOrThrow`/`get` mockImplementation blocks for the configuration service while other specs use `FakeConfigurationService`; review asked for the fake for consistency ("unclear why in one test it's mocked and in another it's faked"). Use `FakeConfigurationService` for config in tests unless a spec genuinely needs call assertions.

title: KMS reused the shared root AWS credentials instead of prefixed per-feature vars
learning: Field-encryption KMS initially keyed off the shared root `AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID`/`AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY`, coupling it to targeted messaging's credentials; the fix introduced dedicated `KMS_AWS_*` vars, matching the existing `SES_AWS_*`/`CSV_AWS_*` pattern. Per-feature AWS credentials use prefixed env vars — an operator rotating one service's creds must not surprise three services.

title: Encryption rollout: uniqueness lost while disabled, serial KMS calls, unguarded down-migration
learning: The email-encryption rollout initially dropped the plaintext unique index unconditionally (two concurrent inserts of the same email could both succeed while the feature was off), decrypted list emails serially (O(N) KMS round-trips per members read), encrypted backfill rows serially inside a transaction holding `DISABLE TRIGGER USER`, threw unattributable decrypt errors, and had a `down()` that would explode on residual ciphertext. All fixed: complementary partial unique indexes so uniqueness holds in both modes, `Promise.all` for list decrypts, bounded-concurrency encrypt outside the transaction, `userId` in decrypt errors, and a guarded reverse migration. When a flag switches storage format, every invariant (uniqueness, error attribution, migration reversibility) must hold in both modes, and independent KMS calls are parallelized.
